mnemonics: Think of Increased as a rising thermometer. The 'I's stand for: I - Implement efficient hardware, I - Innovate with software, I - Involve lifecycle thinking, I - Inspire user change, I - Impact measure and report.
keyPoints: Increased energy efficiency through hardware and data center optimization
Increased focus on the full lifecycle of IT equipment (from manufacturing to disposal)
Increased use of IT to enable sustainability in other sectors (e.g., smart grids, remote work)
commonMistakes: Assuming 'Green IT' only refers to recycling old hardware
Focusing solely on initial manufacturing and ignoring the larger energy consumption during the use phase
Believing the environmental cost of digital data and cloud storage is negligible
